Project.name: MINT Runtime
Project.annotation: Model-Independent Normalization Toolkit — Claude-quality document generation for any LLM. Self-contained runtime with skill prompts, execution engine, validation, QA, and GRACE metadata injection.
Project.keywords: docx, pptx, xlsx, OOXML, docx-js, pptxgenjs, exceljs, validation, auto-fix, QA, GRACE, MCP, template-engine, style-extraction, design-tokens, model-agnostic, local-first

Actors.actor-EndUser: User requesting document generation via LibreChat or direct MCP call.
Actors.actor-LLM: Any OpenAI-compatible model (cloud or local) producing JS code or JSON content.
Actors.actor-Operator: Devops managing MINT deployment, rules, templates, and config.
Actors.actor-ExternalConsumer: Any tool or agent reading GRACE metadata from produced documents.

UC-001.Goal: Get a production-quality DOCX/PPTX/XLSX file matching design-token specifications
UC-001.Preconditions: MINT runtime running, model endpoint configured, template or skill selected
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: File passes L1 validation; no P0 errors; style fingerprint matches or drift logged

UC-002.Action: Writes JS code (frontier/medium) or JSON content (small models)
UC-002.Goal: Produce executable input for MINT runtime
UC-002.Preconditions: Skill prompt selected by tier; design tokens loaded
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Code runs without sandbox violation within 30s; JSON validates against schema

UC-003.Action: Validates an existing document against rules
UC-003.Goal: Identify OOXML errors, style drift, or structural violations
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Validation report lists all violations with rule IDs, severity, and fix hints

UC-004.Action: Extracts design tokens from an existing document
UC-004.Goal: Create a design-tokens.json for reuse as model constraints and validation baseline
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: design-tokens.json contains colors, typography, layout; user can review and edit

UC-005.Action: Runs visual QA on a generated document
UC-005.Goal: Detect rendering issues invisible to programmatic checks (overlaps, margin violations, font substitution)
UC-005.Preconditions: Gotenberg service running; document file produced
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: QA report with confidence scores; overlap >0.5% = warn, >2% = violation

UC-006.Action: Reads GRACE metadata from a document
UC-006.Goal: Navigate document structure and rules without prior knowledge
UC-006.Preconditions: Document has GRACE Custom XML Parts injected
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: manifest and instructions readable via standard OOXML parsers; existing Custom XML preserved

UC-007.Action: Auto-fixes validation violations in a document
UC-007.Goal: Resolve safe and visual violations automatically; backup before fix; reject destructive ones
UC-007.Preconditions: Document has violations with auto-fix categories classified
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Safe fixes applied silently; visual fixes applied with diff in report; destructive rejected with educational hint; max 3 iterations

NonGoals.item-1: No Claude API dependency — Claude is an external benchmark only.
NonGoals.item-2: No dvejsada or upstream MCP server dependency — MINT is the full pipeline.
NonGoals.item-3: No macro, signature, or document protection support in v0.x.
NonGoals.item-4: No inline formatting drift detection (only structural via StyleFingerprint).
NonGoals.item-5: No hosted SaaS in initial release.
NonGoals.item-6: No L3 multimodal QA as blocking gate — advisory only.

Constraints.constraint-1: Code sandbox: no filesystem access outside output dir, no network, 30s timeout.
Constraints.constraint-2: Single OpenAI-compatible API endpoint — one line in config to switch model.
Constraints.constraint-3: All OOXML manipulation must preserve existing Custom XML Parts from other vendors.
Constraints.constraint-4: Three-container deployment: LibreChat + MINT + Gotenberg (plus optional Ollama).
Constraints.constraint-5: GRACE namespace: urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest — must not collide with existing namespaces.
Constraints.constraint-6: RAM budget: ~4GB without local model; ~8GB with 7B; ~48GB with 70B quantized.

Risks.risk-1: GLM-5.1 or other frontier models may produce invalid docx-js/pptxgenjs code in >50% of tasks (G0 kill criterion).
Risks.risk-2: 7B models may fail to fill JSON templates reliably (G0 kill criterion).
Risks.risk-3: Code execution sandbox escape — restricted by no fs, no net, timeout.
Risks.risk-4: Validation false positives may block legitimate documents — mitigated by severity modes.
Risks.risk-5: Skill prompts may not close the quality gap — mitigated by template-first fallback.
Risks.risk-6: Anthropic open-sources full pipeline — different market positioning (local-first, model-agnostic).

OpenQuestions.question-1: Which models to include in G0 hypothesis testing beyond GLM-5.1 and Qwen-7B?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should template engine support Jinja2-style expressions or only simple placeholder substitution?
OpenQuestions.question-3: What is the minimum viable set of builtin templates for launch?
